Now the way that we actually use these modalities in anchoring is to take the picture in your mind's eye and use these submodalities to alter it so it becomes more powerful to you emotionally. For example, if you focus on the image right now in your mind's eye, that image of when you were absolutely certain, you could instruct your brain, now watch this, right? You could instruct your brain to make the picture bigger and make it brighter and make it sharper and you can even move this image closer to you. And by doing that, it will tend to amplify whatever feeling the picture gives you, which in this particular case, right, is a feeling of what? Absolute certainty. However, as it is in real life, there's only so far that a still picture can take you, which is why, now this is interesting, why the feelings that we get from watching a movie or a TV show are typically more intense than from looking to a stack of pictures or from through a magazine. In fact, here's a great analogy. You can look through the evolution of the motion picture business in general. It serves as a perfect metaphor for the relation between the size and the quality of an image that we see and the emotions that we feel as a result of them. For example, in the motion pictures, you start with silent movies, shot in black and white, which were replaced by then talking pictures, shot in black and white, which were then replaced by movie shot in color, which were replaced by movie shot in technical color and stereo, which were replaced by movie shot in widescreen and Dolby sound and then IMAX and then 3D. You get it? Now the obvious trend here is towards making things bigger and brighter and clearer and more realistic. Well, this is exactly how we use the five sensory modalities to intensify our state of certainty by taking the image you created in your mind's eye and putting it through that same evolution of a process as the motion picture industry. I'll guide you through that right now. I want you to start by taking that still picture that you created in your mind's eye and simply putting it into motion so you can actually see yourself moving in the picture and being your absolute best self as you go about closing that huge sale. Now if it helps, you can even put a frame around the picture and imagine it's on a flat screen TV. The point is that by turning the still picture into a motion picture, you're starting to feel more connected to the scene and your state of certainty will intensify, especially when we execute our next step, which is to add on dialogue from your memory. So do that right now. Add on the appropriate dialogue as best you can remember and give yourself that same perfect tonality, perfect dialogue that you had that time in the body language that just allowed you to close this really tough sale. By the way, if the dialogue can't be recalled exactly, you don't like it that much, you can create new dialogue, choosing the words that will most empower you and help you trigger the exact state you want, which is absolute certainty. Okay, now, next step. It's time to make your movie bigger and brighter and clearer and move it even closer to you. And even if you want, you can add on 3D to it or any other change. Remember, each change should make the picture seem more real to you, the movie more real to you and also hence increase your level of certainty, which is absolutely crucial. Remember, because you must set your anchor when you're at the absolute pinnacle of your emotional state. Now while the strategy alone won't fully get you there, what it will show you is going to set you up now for the next step of olfactory anchoring. Make sense to you? Okay, great. Now, let's now want the step five, which is to actually set your anchor. Now, what this entails is taking this intense emotional state that you just created from all those little adjustments in your mind's eye, right, and then linking them to a word or a mantra or some external sound like clapping your hands or squeezing your fists, screaming the word yes. And this is where the process breaks down. At least it does for me and pretty much everyone else out there. You see, if you've ever tried to do this, maybe you have in some self-help something, the problem is, is that trying to artificially manufacture a peak state of certainty to a real 10 is impossible to do. You can't wish your way to it. You can't remember your way to it. There is no denying that there's a certain level of certainty you get to in real life when it happens organically. And without that, you simply can't set a really effective anchor. So that's the problem that I ran into with NLP. And again, I respect Richard Baylor and Dr. Miller. I mean, so it's not about that. He's a brilliant guy. But for me and many people in the real world, the ability to set an anchor, which is just such a crucial thing in success, it breaks down because you just can't remember your way to an absolute 10 on the certainty scale. You get it? So that being said, we're about to get to the true beauty of olfactory anchoring. But before we do, I want you to first take a test here, a quiz, because I give you a lot of information. And these are all building blocks to olfactory anchoring. So please go through this next quiz here and really spend time making sure that you lock in those learnings. And again, thanks to Dr. Baylor for really teaching me NLP anchoring, because it serves as the basis for what I create next, which is going to totally blow you away. I'll see you in a few minutes.
